raw_response:
Tool Call: retrieve("Most common cause of recurrent laryngeal nerve palsy")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the detailed review of the literature, thyroid surgery stands out as the most common cause of recurrent laryngeal nerve palsy. Studies consistently show high incidences of nerve injury ranging from 1% to nearly 22% following thyroid and parathyroid surgeries. While bronchogenic carcinoma and mediastinal tumors do contribute significantly, they remain relatively infrequent compared to surgical interventions affecting the same region. Therefore, I reaffirm my agreement with the initial conclusion that \boxed{B} (thyroid surgery) is indeed the most prevalent cause. The robust statistical support from numerous case reviews strengthens this assertion.
